parentQuestion: A patient undergoes surgery to remove a tumor that has been secreting excessive ...

scaffolds.q: Growth hormone is secreted by the pituitary gland into the bloodstream.
scaffolds.explain: The pituitary is your body's master gland — it sits at the base of your brain and secretes multiple hormones into blood. Growth hormone is one of them. Sit with this knowledge:drawn into this: it travels everywhere via circulation, promoting growth in bones and tissues. Ductless delivery for system-wide effects.

scaffolds.q: Tumors can sometimes retain the secretory functions of the tissue they arose from.
scaffolds.explain: This is the moment I live for:. Tumors are often 'functional,' meaning they keep doing what the original tissue did. A pituitary tumor? It secretes hormones. A thyroid tumor? It might make thyroid hormone. they remember their origins, but they've lost the OFF switch. Unregulated function is the problem.
